title: [HOLD Auth/pull/20597] Distance value displays route distance instead of manually updated distance

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Problem When a user manually updates the distance value for a distance expense in Expensify Classic, the New Expensify app still displays the original route distance instead of the updated value. ### Root Cause In [`src/libs/TransactionUtils/getDistanceInMeters.ts`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/libs/TransactionUtils/getDistanceInMeters.ts), the function checks `transaction.routes.route0.distance` first and returns it if present. Only if routes are empty does it fall back to `transaction.comment.customUnit.quantity`. When `UpdateTransaction` is called (e.g. from Expensify Classic to manually update the distance), it updates `comment.customUnit.quantity` but does **not** clear `routes.route0.distance`. Since the route distance has priority, the stale route value is displayed instead of the manually updated value. ### Reproduction Steps 1. Create an expense on New Expensify using Track distance and the map 2. Enter any two waypoints and save the expense 3. Open the same expense on Expensify Classic 4. Manually update the distance value and save the expense 5. Reopen the expense on New Expensify 6. Observe the distance field still shows the original route distance, not the manually updated value ### Expected Behavior The distance field should display the manually updated value from `comment.customUnit.quantity`. ### Proposed Fix Reverse the priority in `getDistanceInMeters`: prefer `comment.customUnit.quantity` when it is set, and only fall back to `routes.route0.distance` when there is no custom unit quantity. ### Related - [Expensify/Web-Expensify#51378 (comment)](https://github.com/Expensify/Web-Expensify/pull/51378#issuecomment-4142887154)
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @FitseTLT

  • Upwork Job ID: 2037585935439351322

Go to Settings > Workspace > [Workspace Name] > Members 2. Click Invite 3. Observe that the personal message retains formatting from a previous invite On New: 1. Go to Workspace > [Workspace Name] > Invite 2. Enter a new email address 3. Select user role and proceed 4. Navigate to invite message settings 5. Observe a generic invite message (previous message not retained) 6. Edit the message (add links/formatting) 7. Send invite Switch back to Classic: 1. Go to Settings > Workspace > [Workspace Name] > Members 2. Click Invite 3. Observe that the personal message now displays raw HTML formatting 4. Send invite → formatting is not rendered correctly ## Expected Result: Invite messages should sync between New and Classic platforms Formatting (links, styling, etc.) should render consistently across both platforms Messages saved in one platform should appear correctly formatted in the other ## Actual Result: New platform does not load previously saved invite messages Messages edited in New appear as raw HTML in Classic Invite emails sent from Classic do not render formatting properly ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/87cd1f0a-c98e-41c2-8097-369895d908b3 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Reproduction blocked) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @joekaufmanexpensify **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1774532029407719) ## Action Performed: 1. Create a pending Expensify Card transaction and place it on its own report. 2. Go to OldDot and verify you see the submit button. 3. Try to submit and verify you see an error saying the report can't be submitted because of the pending transaction. 4. Go to the report in NewDot. ## Expected Result: We show a submit button with the report in NewDot. Clicking it should throw a similar center-state (web)/bottom drawer (mobile) modal error explaining why it can't be submitted. The next step at the top of the report should also explain that the report can't be submitted until all pending transactions post. ## Proposed copy: Next Step: >All transactions are pending. You can’t submit this report until they post in a few days. Error Modal: >**Unable to submit report** >You can't submit this report because all the transactions are pending. They may take a few days to post. ## Actual Result: We hide the submit button in NewDot if a report only has pending Expensify Card transactions and the report next steps make no mention of why the report can't be submitted. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The screenshots for the issue are in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1774532029407719) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

If tester is not from US, use US VPN. 2. Test on production. 3. Open an incognito window 4. Navigate to new.expensify.com (NewDot) (Very important) 5. Open console > Application > Cookies 6. Set the following cookies (check video for instructions) lc-qa = 1, lc-debug=1 7. Refresh the page NOTE: To bypass these conditions (account with expired free trial ) during QA, also set the lc-qa-mocked=1 cookie. This will ignore country and free trial checks STR: 1. Sign in with an account with expired free trial and control workspace 2. Navigate to Workspace settings and add a member (Should have access to the account) 3. Navigate to Reports > Insights 4. Open every tab: "Spend over time", "Top categories", "Top merchants", and "Top spenders" pages ## Expected Result: The banner: "Earn up to 2% cash back on all US purchases" and CTA: "Get started" and "X" to close the banner is present on all Insights tabs. ## Actual Result: The banner: "Earn up to 2% cash back on all US purchases" and CTA: "Get started" and "X" to close the banner is present on 'Top spenders' tab ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d876b1f4-b290-4f52-84d5-7a30ead66091 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

labels.name: Melvin Triage
labels.description: Automatic AI Analysis of an issue

body:
## Description When clicking on a data point from a chart view, the resulting view contains conflicting date filters — both a broad `date:last-12-months` filter and a specific date range filter (e.g., `date>=2025-11-01 date<=2025-11-30`) are applied simultaneously. ## Expected Behavior When clicking a specific data point (e.g., a single month), only the specific date range filter for that data point should be applied. The broader `date:last-12-months` filter should be removed or replaced. ## Current Behavior Both `date:last-12-months` AND the specific date range (e.g., `date>=2025-11-01 date<=2025-11-30`) are present as filters, which is contradictory. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Go to the Home screen 2. View the insights chart (which defaults to last 12 months) 3. Click on a specific month's data point in the chart 4. Observe the filters applied to the resulting table view ## Platform All platforms

title: [$50] Onboarding instructions reference green button but the global-create button is now grey

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
## Description The onboarding task instructions for "Create your first report" tell users to click the green 🟢 button (the FAB/global-create button). However, the global-create button is now **grey**, not green. This is doubly confusing because: 1. The button color in the instructions (green) doesn't match the actual button (grey) 2. Users see the green **Scan** button and think that's what the instructions are referring to A user was [observed in FullStory](https://app.fullstory.com/ui/o-1WN56P-na1/session/7252072050267841215:7252072050267841215!6559457924356192497) reading the instructions and then clicking the green Scan button instead of the grey FAB "+" button. ### Current Behavior The instruction text (in `src/languages/en.ts`) says: 1. Click the 🟢 button 2. Choose *Create report* 3. Click *Add expense* 4. Add your first expense The green circle emoji doesn't match the actual grey global-create button, and it misleads users toward the green Scan button. ### Expected Behavior The instructions should unambiguously guide users to the correct (grey) "+" button. ### Proposed Solution Update the instruction text in `src/languages/en.ts` to replace the green circle emoji (`🟢` / custom `\uE100` emoji) with a plain `+` character. The team has decided to move away from using an image for the button and just use a `+` instead. For example, the instruction should read something like: "Click the **+** button" rather than "Click the 🟢 button". **Key file:** [`src/languages/en.ts`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/languages/en.ts) — the `createReportTask` instruction text. Also check `src/pages/home/ForYouSection/EmptyState.tsx` which renders these instructions. --- _Issue created by MelvinBot on behalf of `dylanexpensify`_
